issues.detail: All annotations are on the English translation text, not the German source. Forms like 'resisted', 'urging', 'offered', 'lead', 'training', 'offspring' are English words. Should annotate German forms: widerstand/widerstehen, Drängen, anbot/anbieten, Stellungen/Stellung, Ausbildung, Sprößlings/Sprössling, etc.

All annotations are on the English translation text, not the German source. Forms like 'Pope', 'Benedict', 'XVI', 'unexpectedly', 'resigned', 'office' are English words. Should annotate German forms: Papst, Benedikt, überraschend, Amt, zurück/zurücktreten.

Several annotations have English lemmas instead of German: Drama->play, Gegenteil->'opposite word', Papst->pope, 'das'->'which (nominative)', 'der'->'that', 'die'->'which'. Lemmas should be German dictionary forms: Drama, Gegenteil, Papst, das, der, die.

All annotations are on the English translation text, not the German source. Forms 'The', 'bear', 'was', 'already', 'old' are English. Should annotate German: Bär/Bär/noun, war/sein/verb, schon/schon/particle, alt/alt/adjective.
Annotation for 'versetzen' has gloss_hint 'move' but in this sentence it means 'to pawn' (sense 3: zum Pfand geben). Hint should be 'pledge' to match the correct sense.

All annotations are on the English translation text, not the German source. Forms 'Last', 'night', 'it', 'was', 'the', 'first', 'time', 'there', 'were', 'tender', 'moments', 'between', 'two' are English. Should annotate German: Gestern/gestern, Abend/Abend, kam/kommen, ersten/erst, Mal/Mal, Zärtlichkeiten/Zärtlichkeit, beiden/beide.

All annotations are on the English translation text, not the German source. Forms 'also', 'need', 'to cook', 'properly', 'sometimes', 'and', 'not', 'just', 'heat up', 'ready-made meals' are English. Should annotate German forms: musst/müssen, auch/auch, mal/mal, gescheit/gescheit, kochen/kochen, Fertiggerichte/Fertiggericht, warmmachen/warmmachen.
Annotations have English lemmas: Rad->bike, gescheit->sensible, können->manage, nicht->not. Lemmas must be German: Rad, gescheit, können, nicht.

All annotations are on the English translation text, not the German source. Forms 'The', 'symbols', 'LOOP', 'which', 'are', 'used', 'in', 'jump', 'commands', 'translated', 'by', 'assembler', etc. are English. Should annotate German forms: Symbole/Symbol, Sprungbefehlen/Sprungbefehl, verwendet/verwenden, Assembler/Assembler, Adresse/Adresse, Instruktionsspeichers/Instruktionsspeicher, etc.

Translation 'He only goes to the sauna to relax.' is wrong. 'spannen' in this context (sense 7: jemanden beobachten, penetrant anstarren) means 'to peep/stare at people'. Correct: 'He only goes to the sauna to peep (at people).'
Index annotation has gloss_hint 'index (reference sign)' but in context (Stichwortverzeichnis eines Buches) should be 'index (register)' to match sense 0.
Index annotation has gloss_hint 'index (reference sign)' but in context (mnemotechnischer Index) should be 'index (register)' to match sense 0.

Index annotation has all 9 sense gloss_ens pipe-separated as gloss_hint. This should be a single disambiguating hint: 'index (subscript/superscript)' (the sentence talks about 'der kleinen 2' - a subscript number).
Index annotation has gloss_hint 'index (reference sign)' but in context (Index librorum prohibitorum) should be 'banned media list' to match sense 4.

Index annotation has gloss_hint 'reference sign' but in context (Rom ihn auf den Index gesetzt = put on the banned list) should be 'banned media list' to match sense 4. Also: 'weiteren' annotation has gloss_hint 'surprising' which is wrong - should be null or 'further'.

Multiple issues: (1) 'Hilfe' lemma is 'help' (English) - should be 'Hilfe'. (2) 'Indizes' gloss_hint is 'foundation' which is wrong - should be 'linguistic measure' to match sense 6. (3) 'Basis' gloss_hint 'foundation' is acceptable.

OB annotation has lemma 'Oberbürgermeister' but in context (military, umgezogen in eine Balka hinter Gumrak - Stalingrad reference) OB means 'Oberbefehlshaber' (commander-in-chief, sense 1). Lemma should be 'OB' and gloss_hint 'commander-in-chief'.

Translation says 'understand the gender relations' for 'Geschlechterverkehr nachvollziehen'. 'Geschlechterverkehr' here is an archaic/literary variant meaning 'sexual intercourse' (Geschlechtsverkehr). Should be: '...and wanted to reenact the sexual intercourse.'

Translation 'the state of the ants' is misleading. 'Staat der Ameisen' in this context means 'the ant colony' (sense 4: Insektenstaat). Should be: 'I have been observing the ant colony at the forest path for three months.'

Translation 'The emperor's state could hardly fit' is misleading. 'Staat des Kaisers' means 'the emperor's retinue/entourage' (sense 6). Should be: 'The emperor's retinue could hardly fit in the rooms of the palace.'
'gefällt' annotation has lemma 'gefallen' (to please) but in context 'Entscheidungen werden gefällt' it is from 'fällen' (to make a decision). Lemma should be 'fällen', pos 'verb'.
No translation (null) and no annotations. Cannot verify.
No annotations present. Translation is acceptable but 'violent-glorifying' should be 'violence-glorifying'.

'Namen' annotation has gloss_hint 'noun' which is nonsensical - should be null or 'name'. Translation 'belong to the index type' is slightly awkward; 'belong to the sign type index' would be more precise.

Ohrekreis is a historical administrative district named after the Ohre river in Saxony-Anhalt. Current synonyms_en 'auditory canal' and 'ear passage' are completely wrong - they confuse 'Ohre' (river name) with 'Ohr' (ear).
'gespannt' annotation has gloss_hint 'stressed' but in this context (Die Leine muss gespannt werden = the line must be stretched taut) it should be 'stretch' or 'pull tight' to match sense 0.
'Zeilen' annotation has gloss_hint 'affection' which is wrong. Zeile means 'line' (of text). Hint should be null or 'line'.
'einer' annotation has lemma 'einer' and gloss_hint 'mother' which is wrong. 'einer' here is the indefinite article genitive feminine of 'ein'. Lemma should be 'ein', pos 'determiner', hint null.
'Er' annotation has lemma 'he' (English). Should be German lemma 'er'.
No annotations. Translation is correct.
No annotations. Translation is correct.
No annotations. Translation is correct.
No annotations. Translation is correct.
